reviews.review_msg: 201 is difficult but he made it easy. Genuinely, just do the practice problems he gave and you will succeed.
Good prof, easy to understand and tests were easy. All I studied was the practice problems and past tests he posted and got an A+.
One of the best professors in the econ department, his tests were definitely one of the easiest among all the other econ courses I took.

The best professor in the whole SFU. It was really the wisest choice for me to take this professor's class in the penultimate semester of graduation. Do not miss this professor if you want to learn about development economics theory and improve your economic writing. This professor is extremely patient and responsible for every student.
He is a very nice person and he'd love to help students during his office hours. The materials are not hard if you already took Econ 355w. Highly recommend for 4-level course!!!!
Best prof.
One of the worst handwriting I've ever seen and there's no slides provided

Super clear lecture. He actually tells you how to solve the problems during lectures, and if you go to his office hours, he will take his time to teach you how to solve them. Make sure you read over the lectures and all his exams are based on the lecture notes. 2 midterms and 1final, tough, but dw, everyone else are "indifferent" (wink wink)

This is the professor that made me decide not to do an Economic Minor and turned me off the area of study. He was incredibly boring, would write illegible mess  on an overhead all class, and brought lots of math into the class which was unnecessary. I do not recommend at all.

His assignments seem easy, the examples in class seem easy, but wait until you get to the midterm and the exams. Hard AF. Go to  your tutorials, do the practice problems, and ask your TA for help. I've never taken such a hard class.  The exams have 5 true or false questions, and 3-4 multi-part long problems.  Good luck.
His tests are really hard, good luck guys.
ECON 201 is a nightmare for me. Hard exams and time consuming assignments.

less math based than other profs but still hard, practice exams are easier than actual exams with curveball questions. He is a good explainer but you don't hear anything he says because you're trying to decode what he's writing . He's friendly enough but doesn't reply to emails and doesn't help out beyond class time and 1 office hour after class

His tests are really hard. Terrible handwriting. He gave you 2 practice finals from 2014 and 2015. And the 2016 final is completely different. Avoid it at all cost. He only have one office hour (1hr) per week.

Great prof.  Perfectly paced lecturesnotes that really help you understand the material-I was rarely bored.  Karaivanov seems like a helpful prof.  As ECON's gatekeeper, I found this course too easy though.  The exam material is very straightforward IMO, finished with an A+.  If you think you're good at BASIC derivative calculus, you'll ace it.

Great Prof. He is very knowledgeable and super nice. Very handsome as well. Easy to understand and you will never fall as sleep in his class. Exams and assignments are fair but not easy (especially the true false section).  However, his handwriting is terrible and he has only one day (1 hour) office hour per week.
excellent professor.

the class is a breeze i never showed up and ended up with a B+, the exams are fair the final was a bit more challenging than the midterm but not impossible. The only bad thing about the class was the TA favoured some students over others and this was a bit annoying but the class was interesting and easy and Alex was a clear prof
Solid class, interesting topics. Highly recommended.

Really great professor. Notes are clear and explains concepts really well so that you don't even need to read the book. Assignments are relatively interesting and aren't too difficult. Midterm and Final's are fair but the quantitative parts involving math are definitely challenging and tricky.
Fair Midterm, but very hard final. Overall, it still worth to take it
I was warned about Econ 301, but I was surprised at how easy this guy taught it. So easy, it seemed to be a repeat of an Econ 100 level course.
Great Prof. Great class. Loved it.
Any shmoe could teach this class.  He did a decent enough job doing it. He wasn't easy, he wasn't hard.  Take his class, get a B, and move on to more interesting courses.
Hard but fair exams. don't understand why others are saying he is easy compare to other profs. did horribly....
yawn
Average. But clearly low enthusiasm for teaching, gives the impression that office hours are an interruption of his "real" work. A bit condescending and impatient.
No Comments
Very Helpful and friendly. His exams are Tough, but Fair. Need to improve his  horrible hand writing. Lecture is kinda dull, but the pace picked up later in the course.

He is one of the best professor in SFU. although second mid-term is pretty hard, but the first mid-term and the final although not very easy, but at least, not hard. I think it's becasue this is a hard course. As i heard, other professor had made them even more harder.
I guess he read too much of "too easy, too easy,..." here. Because now, he gave a very hard midterm. The 1st got avg of 71 but the 2nd got 59.Better watch out and find other ECON 301 prof.
Excellent Professor. Always willing to help out the student so the know the material. Very skilled at presenting the material in an easy manner. Exams are slightly easier than most.
Too easy!  It is like he is asking us to give him easy rating back!
easiest midterm and final
He is really nice guy!!!! lectures were bit boring but informative. I think he is better than any other Econ 301 instructors.
Excellent at explaining the material, however the lectures are extremely dry. Don't take a night class with him.
Exams are challenging.  Needs to work on his lecture! Pretty boring! I recommend to not take him.
WORST LECTURER!

Took his course in the U of Chicago.  This russian guy has absolutely no knowledge of the intermed. econ.  The textbook is brutally error-proned! Avoid! Take J. Amegashie in Econ301 if you want good grade and lecture!
